owner_boundary.summary: Lode declares stable failure class meaning and consumer hints. Core owns final envelope fields, and App owns final presentation.

classes.trigger: Package manifest, schema, lifecycle, post-check, resource, fixture, or failure mapping contract is malformed or contradicts package identity.
classes.recovery_hint: Repair the package asset contract before admission or execution.

classes.trigger: Current Harbor facts cannot satisfy the required BOSS Zhipin browser, page, source-ref, or evidence-ref profile.
classes.owner: WebEnvoy Core admission with Harbor fact inputs
classes.recovery_hint: Open a supported BOSS Zhipin page in the bound browser and request fresh Harbor facts.

classes.trigger: The BOSS Zhipin route, wapi response shape, source refs, or field mapping no longer matches fixture and post-check expectations.
classes.owner: Lode package maintainer after Core/Harbor detects source or mapping drift
classes.recovery_hint: Refresh source shape, fixture, normalizer, or post-check requirements without storing live raw evidence in Lode.

classes.trigger: A valid read produces no job cards or detail fields for the requested target.
classes.owner: WebEnvoy Core result projection from Lode output classification
classes.recovery_hint: Surface an empty state; do not treat it as package or runtime failure.

classes.trigger: The page shows a login wall or the current identity is not logged in.
classes.owner: WebEnvoy Core admission with Harbor page facts
classes.recovery_hint: Ask the user to log in manually in the bound browser profile, then retry.

classes.trigger: The current BOSS identity cannot access job seeker read surfaces required by this package.
classes.owner: WebEnvoy Core admission with Harbor identity facts
classes.recovery_hint: Ask the user to switch to a BOSS job seeker identity in the bound browser.

classes.trigger: The page shows CAPTCHA, verification, abnormal traffic, or risk-control prompt.
classes.owner: Harbor page facts consumed by Core
classes.recovery_hint: Stop automation and ask the user to resolve verification manually; do not bypass it.

classes.trigger: The SPA, route, wapi source, result grid, or detail container is unavailable in the current execution window.
classes.owner: WebEnvoy Core runtime read using Harbor page facts
classes.recovery_hint: Wait for page hydration or refresh the target route before retrying.

classes.trigger: A detail read lacks the securityId emitted by job-search.
classes.owner: WebEnvoy Core input projection from Lode follow-up refs
classes.recovery_hint: Use a detail_ref emitted by job-search.

classes.trigger: A search read lacks a non-empty query.
The requested city or filter code cannot be resolved to a supported BOSS value.
classes.recovery_hint: Use a supported city or filter code.

classes.trigger: The request asks for batch pagination beyond the first page package boundary.
classes.recovery_hint: Use the first page package only; batch pagination requires a later Work Item.

classes.trigger: The selected job is offline, deleted, hidden, or no longer returned by the detail endpoint.
classes.owner: WebEnvoy Core result projection from BOSS source status
classes.recovery_hint: Surface job unavailable and ask the user to choose another search result.

classes.trigger: The current identity or page lacks permission to read the requested job detail.
classes.owner: WebEnvoy Core admission or runtime read
classes.recovery_hint: Ask the user to open BOSS manually with an identity allowed to view the job.

classes.trigger: A required public field such as job title, company, location, salary, recruiter, or description is absent.
classes.recovery_hint: Treat as partial result or repair package mapping after fresh evidence refs are available.

classes.trigger: Network or browser resource facts report a failed read, blocked request, stale source ref, or missing evidence ref.
classes.owner: WebEnvoy Core with Harbor evidence facts
classes.recovery_hint: Request fresh browser, source, and evidence refs; do not store raw runtime material in Lode.

consumer_boundary.core: Core may consume category, outcome_hint, retryable, and requires_user_action as compatibility hints, but Core owns the final envelope schema.
consumer_boundary.app: App may consume display and severity as presentation hints, but App owns final UI copy.
consumer_boundary.lode: Lode validator checks identity, required classes, and forbidden fields only; it does not execute runtime matching.

non_goals: No live runtime matching, external writes, credentials, production payloads, or raw evidence bodies.
